content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

August 8, 2007

Stephen E. Miklos
Councilmember City of Folsom
50 Natoma Street
Folsom, California 95630

RE: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-07-125

Dear Mr. Miklos:

This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding Section 84308 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹ Please note that our advice is based solely on the facts presented in your request for advice. The Commission does not act as a finder of fact when providing advice. (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71.)

QUESTION

Does Section 84308 prevent you from participating in upcoming proceedings before the Sacramento County Local Agency Formation Committee regarding the Natomas Panhandle project, even though you returned contributions from persons associated with that project within 30 days following actual notice of that connection?

CONCLUSION

As we understand your account of the facts, you did not know or have reason to know that three of your campaign contributors were participants or agents of participants in the proceeding at issue, until at least July 2, 2007. Your action to return contributions of more than $250 was therefore timely under Section 84308, which as a result does not prohibit your participation in that proceeding.

¹ Government Code sections 81000-91014. Commission regulations appear at title 2, sections 18109-18997, of the California Code of Regulations. All references to code "Sections" shall be to the Government Code, and all references to "Regulations" shall be to title 2 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise noted.

File No. A-07-125
Page No. 2

FACTS

You are an elected member of the Folsom City Council, and serve by reason of
that office as a representative on the Board of the Sacramento County Local Agency
Formation committee ("LAFCO"). You began your service on LAFCO as an alternate on
May 20, 2004, and became a regular representative on March 29, 2007. Your current
term will expire in May, 2008. An application is currently pending before LAFCO to
annex to the City of Sacramento certain real property in the county's unincorporated area.
This property is known as the Natomas Panhandle; the application is being pursued by
Dunmore Homes and Vaquero Land Holdings ("Developers"), who are represented by
the Law Offices of George Phillips. You understand that this annexation may have a
hearing before LAFCO in September, 2007.

On July 2, 2007 Mr. Phillips' office notified you by telephone that members of
the Developers' team had contributed to your City Council campaign. Specifically, you
report to us four contributions in the amount of $150.00; one on October 16, 2006 and a
second on April 30, 2007 from MacKay & Somps; a single contribution on October 26,
2006 from JDA/Jeff DeMure, and a single contribution on November 11, 2006 from
George Phillips. You indicate that it was this phone call that first alerted you to the fact
that these contributors were agents of a party with an application that would come before
the LAFCO Board.² You were previously aware that you had received contributions
from these persons, and knew of the annexation project, but you did not know that these
contributors were connected to the annexation project until the July 2 phone call from
Mr. Phillips' office.

On July 23, 2007 your campaign wrote three checks whereby you returned the full
amount of the contributions from Mr. Phillips and JDA/Jeff DeMure; with the third check
for $51.00 you reduced the total amount received from MacKay & Somps to $249.00.
These contributions were returned less than 30 days after Mr. Phillips' office notified you
of the connection between these contributors and the annexation project, but more than
30 days after you accepted the contributions.

ANALYSIS

Section 84308 bars any officer of a public agency, who is running or has run for
elective office, from participating in decisions affecting his or her campaign contributors.
Specifically, the law disqualifies an officer from participating in certain proceedings if he
or she has received campaign contributions of more than $250 from a party, a participant,
or their agents within the 12 months preceding the decision, and requires disclosure on
the record of the proceeding of all campaign contributions received from these persons

² You state that you were advised (apparently either during or after the July 2 telephone conversation) that
each of these contributors is a representative of or counsel to the Developers, and are involved in the
annexation. You also state that you assume (at least for purposes of this letter) that these contributors are
"agents" of the Developers within the meaning of Regulation 18438.3(a).

File No. A-07-125
Page No. 3

during that period. However, under the second paragraph of Section 84308(c), the officer
may participate in the proceeding if he or she returns the contribution, or that portion of it
that exceeds $250.00. The paragraph in question reads as follows:

"If an officer receives a contribution which would otherwise
require disqualification under this section, returns (sic) the contribution
within 30 days from the time he or she knows, or should have known,
about the contribution and the proceeding involving a license, permit, or
other entitlement for use, he or she shall be permitted to participate in the
proceeding."

This option is open only for 30 days after the officer knows, or should have
known, of both the pending proceeding and the contribution. Your question presumes the
application of Section 84308, and simply asks if you returned the contributions in time to
permit you to participate in LAFCO proceedings on the Natomas Panhandle project.

Regulation 18438.7 interprets the governing statute as follows:

"For purposes of Government Code Section 84308:
(a) An officer knows, or has reason to know that a person has a financial
interest in the decision in a proceeding if:
(1) The person is a party; or
(2) The person is a participant and reveals facts in his or her written or
oral support or opposition before the agency which makes the person's
financial interest apparent;
(b) An officer knows or should have known about a proceeding pending
before the agency if either:
(1) The officer has received notice of the license, permit or other
entitlement proceeding. Notice includes receipt of an agenda or docket
identifying the proceeding and the party or other persons affected by
name; or
(2) The officer has actual knowledge of the proceeding.
(c) An officer knows, or should have known about a contribution if:
(1) The contribution has been disclosed by the party pursuant to Section
84308(d); or
(2) The officer has actual knowledge of the contribution."

You indicate that you knew you had received contributions from the persons in
question before July 2, 2007, and that you also knew of the Natomas Panhandle project
before that date. Your account of the facts indicates that none of the contributors to your
campaign are parties to the proceeding, which would have disqualified you under
subdivision (a)(1) above. We are left to consider subdivision (a)(2) of the regulation.

File No. A-07-125
Page No. 4

If you learned only on or after July 2, 2007 that these known contributors were
participants or agents involved in this proceeding, you had actual knowledge of their
involvement less than 30 days before you returned their contributions. If any of these
participants or agents had revealed facts at an earlier date in a written or oral presentation
to LAFCO which made a financial interest in the decision apparent, you might have had
"reason to know" of the financial interest prior to July 2, 2007. But your account of the
facts does not reveal any such presentations, and we therefore assume that none were
made. On this assumption, we conclude that until July 2, 2007 you did not have reason to
know that these contributors were participants or agents of participants in the Natomas
Panhandle project. Your return of the contributions twenty one days later thus qualifies
you to take part in the proceeding under the "return" provision in the second paragraph of
Section 84308(c).

Please bear in mind that Section 84308 also prohibits an officer from accepting,
soliciting or directing a contribution over $250 from a party or participant to a proceeding
(or their agents) for three months after the final decision in the proceeding. The statutory
prohibition applies to any officer of the agency before which the proceeding was pending.
No exception is made for an officer who recused himself or herself from the proceeding.
(See, e.g. the Boznanski Advice Letter, No. A-00-172.)

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Scott Hallabrin
General Counsel

By: Lawrence T. Woodlock
Senior Counsel, Legal Division

LTW:jgl
